info.title: Aionvision API
info.description: API for detecting hallucinations in Vision Language Model outputs

post.description:
Login with email and password.

Creates a new session and sets an HttpOnly cookie with the session token.

Refresh the current session by extending its expiry.

Returns new session information with updated expiry time.

Logout current session or all sessions.

Revokes the session in the database and clears the cookie.

get.description:
Get current authenticated user.

Validates the session and returns user information.

post.description:
Register a new user.

Creates user, tenant, and optionally a session for immediate login.

Login with Google OAuth.

Validates Google ID token and creates a session.

Initiate Google OAuth flow with CSRF protection.

Returns authorization URL with state parameter for CSRF protection.
The state must be validated in the callback to prevent CSRF attacks.

Args:
    action: OAuth flow action - "login", "register", or "link"

Returns:
    Dictionary with authorization_url and state token

schema.title: Response Initiate Google Oauth Api V2 Auth Session Google Initiate Post

get.description:
Google OAuth callback handler for server-side authorization code flow.

Handles the authorization code from Google OAuth and exchanges it for tokens.
This endpoint is called by Google after user authorizes the application.

SECURITY: Validates the state parameter to prevent CSRF attacks.
List all active sessions for the current user.

schema.title: Response List Sessions Api V2 Auth Session Sessions Get

post.description:
Request a password reset.

Always returns success to prevent email enumeration.

get.description: Validate a password reset token.

post.description:
Complete password reset with new password.

Automatically logs in the user after successful reset.

Verify user email with verification token.

Validates the verification token and marks the user's email as verified.

Securely link an OAuth provider to existing account.

Requires user to be authenticated (valid session).
Optional: Can require password verification for extra security.

Resend verification email to the current user.

Requires user to be authenticated but allows unverified users.

Resend verification email by email address (unauthenticated).

Allows users to request verification email resend without being logged in.
Returns success regardless of whether the email exists (to prevent enumeration).

get.description:
Check the current user's email verification status.

Returns information about whether the user's email is verified and
when verification was last sent.

post.description:
Rotate the current session token.

This endpoint creates a new session with a new token and revokes the current session.
Used after security-sensitive operations like password changes to prevent session fixation.

The new session token is returned in an HttpOnly cookie.

Create a new API key for the user's tenant.

The raw API key is only returned once and cannot be retrieved again.
Requires ADMIN permission on the tenant.

get.description:
List all API keys for the user's tenant.

Returns API key metadata without the raw keys.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

Get details of a specific API key.

Users can only access API keys from their own tenant.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

put.description:
Update an existing API key.

Only provided fields will be updated. Metadata will be merged with existing metadata.
Users can only update API keys from their own tenant.
Requires ADMIN permission on the tenant.

delete.description:
Delete (deactivate) an API key.

API keys are not physically deleted but marked as inactive.
Users can only delete API keys from their own tenant.
Requires ADMIN permission on the tenant.

post.description:
**⚠️ DEPRECATED:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use the upload flow instead,
    which handles file upload and description in a single operation. This endpoint
    will be removed in a future version.

    ---

    Submit a batch of images for description processing.
    This endpoint creates a batch job and immediately queues all items
    for background processing by workers. Returns batch ID for status tracking.
    Requires EDIT permission on the tenant.

get.description:
Get the current status of a batch operation.

Returns real-time status including progress, timing estimates,
and processing statistics.

Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

delete.description:
Cancel a running batch operation.

Only pending or processing batches can be cancelled.
This will stop queueing new items and mark the batch as cancelled,
but items already being processed will complete.

Requires EDIT permission on the tenant.

get.description:
Get the results of a batch operation.

Results are paginated and can optionally include or exclude failed items.
Provides comprehensive data including processing times, error details,
and result metadata.

Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.
Retrieve paginated history of image descriptions with filtering and search capabilities

post.description: Export description results in various formats

get.description: Check the status of an export operation
Retrieve a stored image associated with a processed image

parameters.name: thumbnail

schema.description: Return thumbnail by default for optimal performance
schema.title: Thumbnail

parameters.description: Return thumbnail by default for optimal performance

parameters.name: size

schema.description: Thumbnail size if thumbnail=true (tiny, small, medium)
schema.title: Size

parameters.description: Thumbnail size if thumbnail=true (tiny, small, medium)

get.description: Retrieve a stored image associated with a batch item

parameters.name: thumbnail

schema.description: Return thumbnail by default for optimal performance
schema.title: Thumbnail

parameters.description: Return thumbnail by default for optimal performance

parameters.name: size

schema.description: Thumbnail size if thumbnail=true (tiny, small, medium)
schema.title: Size

parameters.description: Thumbnail size if thumbnail=true (tiny, small, medium)

get.description: Get a list of all available size variants for an image
Retrieve a specific size variant of an image

Get a thumbnail image for a batch item.

This endpoint retrieves the appropriate image variant based on the requested size.

Sizes:
- tiny: 64px max dimension (tiny_64 variant)
- small: 256px max dimension (small_256 variant) [default]
- medium: 750px max dimension (medium_750 variant)

Returns the image file with proper caching headers.
Access is restricted to the tenant that owns the batch item.

Get a thumbnail image for a processed image.

This is a convenience endpoint that automatically selects the appropriate
image variant based on the requested size.

Sizes:
- tiny: 64px max dimension (tiny_64 variant)
- small: 256px max dimension (small_256 variant) [default]
- medium: 750px max dimension (medium_750 variant)

Returns the image file with proper caching headers.
Access is restricted to the tenant that owns the image.

Check if user has enough quota for an upload BEFORE starting.

This lightweight endpoint allows the frontend to validate quota
before adding optimistic entries to the UI. Returns remaining quota
and whether the upload can proceed.

Returns:
    - can_proceed: Whether the upload can proceed
    - requested: Number of files requested
    - available: Number of uploads remaining
    - monthly_limit: Total monthly upload limit
    - current_usage: Current month's usage
    - message: User-friendly message if quota exceeded

post.description:
Check which file hashes already exist for this tenant.

Used by the frontend to detect duplicate files before uploading.
Compares SHA-256 content hashes against existing non-deleted images.

get.description:
Get unified status for a single image upload.

This endpoint provides a single source of truth by combining status
information from images, upload_intents, and task_intents tables.

Returns:
    UnifiedImageStatusResponse with consolidated status information

Get unified status summary for a batch.

Provides aggregated counts and overall status for all images
in the batch without loading individual image data.

Returns:
    BatchStatusSummaryResponse with aggregated status information

Find uploads stuck in non-terminal states.

Useful for identifying images that may need manual intervention
or investigation.

Args:
    stuck_minutes: Minutes before considering an upload stuck (default: 30)
    limit: Maximum results to return (default: 100)

Returns:
    StuckUploadsResponse with list of stuck images

post.description: Generate a presigned URL for uploading a document
Confirm upload and trigger document processing

get.description: Get processing status of a document

Check if user has enough quota for a document upload BEFORE starting.

This lightweight endpoint allows the frontend to validate quota
before adding optimistic entries to the UI. Returns remaining quota
and whether the upload can proceed.

Returns:
    - can_proceed: Whether the upload can proceed
    - requested: Number of documents requested
    - available: Number of uploads remaining
    - monthly_limit: Total monthly upload limit
    - current_usage: Current month's usage
    - message: User-friendly message if quota exceeded

post.description: Generate presigned URLs for batch document upload
Confirm batch upload and trigger document extraction processing

get.description: Get the status of a document batch upload

post.description: Search document chunks by semantic similarity

get.description: List all documents for the tenant

parameters.name: status_filter

schema.description: Filter by extraction status (pending, completed, failed)
schema.title: Status Filter

parameters.description: Filter by extraction status (pending, completed, failed)

get.description: Get information about a specific document

delete.description: Delete a document, its S3 file, and cascade delete all chunks

patch.description: Update document metadata (title and tags). Only provided fields will be updated.

get.description: Get full extracted text from a document
Get all chunks for a document

post.description:
Delete multiple documents in a single operation.

    **Performance Benefits:**
    - Single API call instead of N individual calls
    - Batch S3 deletion (up to 1000 objects per request)
    - Optimized database operations

    **Behavior:**
    - Documents currently processing (text extraction or embedding) are skipped
    - Successfully processes as many documents as possible
    - Returns detailed results for each document

    **Limits:**
    - Maximum 100 documents per batch
    - All documents must belong to the authenticated tenant

get.description: Download the original document file via presigned S3 URL

post.description:
Check if an operation would exceed usage limits.

This endpoint allows clients to check before attempting an operation
whether they have sufficient credits available.

get.description:
Get usage summary for the authenticated tenant.

Returns aggregated usage statistics for the specified period.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

Get detailed usage records for the authenticated tenant.

Returns individual usage events with pagination.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

Get usage data grouped by time period.

Returns time-series data for charts and analytics.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

Get detailed plan utilization and subscription information.

Returns comprehensive plan usage data including current utilization,
billing period information, and optimization recommendations.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

Get top endpoints by usage count.

Returns the most frequently used endpoints for analytics.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

Get a breakdown of credit usage for the current billing period.

Returns total credits used, monthly limit, remaining credits,
usage percentage, and the reset date.

Get monthly token usage breakdown for the authenticated tenant.

Returns token counts split by operation type (describe, chat)
for the specified month.

Get per-file token usage for the authenticated tenant.

Returns token usage for each file that has been processed with auto-describe,
sorted by most recent first.

Get per-conversation token usage for the authenticated tenant.

Returns token usage aggregated by chat session, sorted by most recent first.

Get dashboard analytics from real activity data.

Queries processing_history, chat_messages, images, chat_sessions,
and image_descriptions to build a comprehensive activity overview.

Get current user profile information.

Returns the authenticated user's profile data including preferences.

patch.description:
Update user profile information.

Allows updating name and preferences.

post.description:
Change user password.

Requires current password for verification.
Rate limited to prevent brute force attacks.

schema.title: Response Change Password Api V2 User Change Password Post

post.description:
Request email change.

Requires password verification and sends confirmation email to new address.

Confirm a pending email change using the verification token.

The token is sent to the new email address when the change is requested.

get.description:
Get all active sessions for the current user.

Returns a list of all active sessions with device and location info.

schema.title: Response Get User Sessions Api V2 User Sessions Get

delete.description:
Revoke a specific session.

Marks the session as inactive, effectively logging out that device.

post.description:
Revoke all sessions except the current one.

Useful for security purposes when user suspects account compromise.

Allow OAuth users to add password authentication.

This endpoint enables users who signed up with OAuth (Google, GitHub, etc.)
to set a password, allowing them to sign in with either method.

Returns:
    Dict with success status and updated auth_provider

Raises:
    400: If user already has a password set
    401: If not authenticated
    500: Server error

schema.title: New Password
schema.description: Password must be at least 8 characters

schema.title: Response Set Initial Password Api V2 User Set Initial Password Post

post.description:
Permanently delete the authenticated user's account.

Deletes all owned tenants (cascading to API keys, members, etc.),
cancels active Stripe subscriptions, and creates an audit record.

Requires the user to type 'delete my account' as confirmation.
Rate limited to 3 attempts per day.

get.description:
Get current tenant settings.

Returns configuration and usage information for the tenant.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

patch.description:
Update tenant settings.

Requires ADMIN role on the tenant.

get.description:
Get tenant usage limits and current usage.

Returns limits, current usage, and remaining quota.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

Get tenant members.

Returns list of users who have access to this tenant.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

schema.title: Response Get Tenant Members Api V2 Tenant Members Get

post.description:
Invite a user to join the tenant.

Requires ADMIN role on the tenant (OWNER check will be done within the function).
Creates a new user account if the email doesn't exist.

patch.description:
Update a tenant member's role.

Requires ADMIN role on the tenant. Permission validation is handled by the use case.
Cannot change the owner's role.

delete.description:
Remove a member from the tenant.

Requires ADMIN role on the tenant (OWNER check will be done within the function).
Cannot remove the owner.

get.description:
Get status of custom S3 configuration.

Returns configuration status without exposing sensitive credentials.
Requires VIEW permission on the tenant.

post.description:
Configure custom S3 bucket for the tenant.

The tenant's S3 credentials are validated by testing bucket access,
then encrypted and stored securely. Once configured, uploads can
specify storage_target='custom' to use this bucket.

Requires ADMIN permission on the tenant.

delete.description:
Remove custom S3 configuration.

After removal, all uploads will use the default Aionvision bucket.
Requires ADMIN permission on the tenant.

post.description:
Validate that custom S3 configuration is still working.

Tests bucket access using stored credentials without modifying anything.
Useful for health checks and debugging.

get.description:
Get audit logs for the tenant.

Returns a paginated list of audit log entries with filtering options.
Requires ADMIN role on the tenant.

Get a single audit log entry.

Requires ADMIN role on the tenant.

post.description: Create a Stripe checkout session for subscription upgrade.
Create a Stripe billing portal session for subscription management.

get.description: Get current subscription status for user.

parameters.name: tenant_id

schema.description: Tenant UUID. If omitted, uses primary owned tenant (deprecated)
schema.title: Tenant Id

parameters.description: Tenant UUID. If omitted, uses primary owned tenant (deprecated)

post.description: Update existing subscription (upgrade, downgrade, cancel, or resume).
Handle Stripe webhook events.
Purchase a prepaid credit pack.

get.description: Get prepaid credit balance and purchase history.

parameters.name: tenant_id

schema.description: Tenant UUID to get credit balance for
schema.title: Tenant Id

parameters.description: Tenant UUID to get credit balance for

post.description:
Create a new chat session for image-informed conversations.

    Optionally provide initial images to include in the context.
    The session will track token usage and manage conversation history.

get.description: Get a list of user's chat sessions with metadata. Optionally search by title or message content.

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Search sessions by title or message content
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Search sessions by title or message content

parameters.name: min_messages

schema.description: Filter sessions with at least this many messages
schema.title: Min Messages

parameters.description: Filter sessions with at least this many messages

get.description: Get a chat session with all messages and context.

delete.description: Close an active chat session and release resources.

post.description:
Send a message in a chat session and receive an AI response.

    The AI will have access to the context of selected images and
    previous conversation history. Token usage is tracked and limited.

put.description:
Update the selected images for a chat session.

    This changes the context available to the AI for subsequent messages.
    Maximum 1000 images can be selected at once.

Update the selected documents for a chat session.

    This sets which documents are available in the chat context.
    Maximum 100 documents can be selected at once.

Update the selected videos for a chat session.

    This sets which videos are available in the chat context for VideoAnalysisAgent.
    Maximum 100 videos can be selected at once.

patch.description:
Update the session mode (all images vs selected images).

    This endpoint allows toggling between:
    - All images mode: Uses all available images in context
    - Selected images mode: Uses only specifically selected images

post.description:
Approve or cancel an execution plan.

    When a complex multi-topic request is made, the system may return a plan
    requiring approval. Use this endpoint to approve or cancel that plan.

    For 'approve' action, include the plan_data from the original response.

202.description: Plan execution accepted, progress streamed via WebSocket

post.description:
Search for images using natural language queries.

    Examples:
    - "red cars from last week"
    - "images with dogs and cats"
    - "high confidence detections of people"

    The search engine understands context, synonyms, and filters.

patch.description: Update the title of an existing chat session

get.description:
Export a chat session in various formats.

    Supported formats:
    - json: Complete session data with metadata
    - markdown: Human-readable markdown format
    - text: Plain text format

parameters.name: format

schema.description: Export format: json, markdown, or text
schema.title: Format

parameters.description: Export format: json, markdown, or text

post.description:
Send a message to the chat session and receive streaming response.

    Uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream the response token by token.

parameters.name: debug_reasoning

schema.description: Include agent reasoning in thinking_step events (debug only)
schema.title: Debug Reasoning

parameters.description: Include agent reasoning in thinking_step events (debug only)

get.description: Get all images for the authenticated user for chat context

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of images to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of images to return

get.description:
List user's uploaded files with filtering and search capabilities.

    Supports:
    - Full-text search with multiple modes (all, metadata, visible text)
    - Filtering by tags
    - Date range filtering
    - Pagination

parameters.name: search_mode

schema.description: Search mode: all, metadata, or visible_text

parameters.description: Search mode: all, metadata, or visible_text

parameters.name: media_types

schema.description: Filter by media types: image, video, document
schema.title: Media Types

parameters.description: Filter by media types: image, video, document

parameters.name: folder_id

schema.description: Filter by folder ID (returns files in this folder only)
schema.title: Folder Id

parameters.description: Filter by folder ID (returns files in this folder only)

parameters.name: sort_by

schema.description: Sort field: created_at, content_created_at, title, size_bytes
schema.title: Sort By

parameters.description: Sort field: created_at, content_created_at, title, size_bytes

post.description:
Upload file directly via streaming (bypasses presigned URL flow).

    Benefits:
    - Eliminates S3 download bottleneck (2-5 second savings per file)
    - Processes file in-memory while uploading to S3 (parallel execution)
    - Suitable for files <100MB

get.description:
Get detailed information about a specific user file.

    Accepts either:
    - Full UUID (e.g., 67d1d598-1234-5678-9abc-def012345678)
    - 8-character prefix (e.g., 67d1d598) - will resolve to full ID

    Includes:
    - Full metadata
    - All descriptions
    - Processing history
    - URLs for different sizes

patch.description:
Update file metadata (title and tags).

    Only provided fields will be updated.

delete.description:
Soft delete a user file.

    The file can be recovered within 30 days.

post.description:
Delete multiple user files in a single operation.

    **Performance Benefits:**
    - Single API call instead of N individual calls
    - Batch S3 deletion (up to 1000 objects per request)
    - Single cache invalidation at the end

    **Behavior:**
    - Files currently processing are skipped (not failed)
    - Successfully processes as many files as possible
    - Returns detailed results for each file

    **Limits:**
    - Maximum 100 files per batch
    - All files must belong to the authenticated user

Save a website link for later reference.

    The link will be crawled asynchronously to extract:
    - Open Graph metadata (title, description, image)
    - Page favicon
    - Page content for search

    Duplicate URLs are detected but allowed (with warning).

Manually trigger a recrawl of a link to refresh its metadata.

    The link will be re-fetched and Open Graph data, favicon, and page
    content will be extracted again.

    Rate limited to once per hour per link.

schema.title: Response Recrawl Link Api V2 User Files  Link Id  Recrawl Post

400.description: Not a link or already processing

get.description:
Get a specific variant of a user file image.

    Returns a redirect to a presigned URL for direct download from S3.

    Available variants:
    - original: Original uploaded image
    - tiny_64: 64px max dimension
    - small_256: 256px max dimension
    - medium_750: 750px max dimension
    - large_1024: 1024px max dimension

Download the original user file.

    Returns a redirect to a presigned URL for direct download from S3
    with proper Content-Disposition headers for file download.

post.description:
Manually trigger variant generation for an image.

This endpoint is useful for:
- Recovering stuck images
- Retrying failed variant generation
- Debugging variant generation issues

schema.title: Response Trigger Variant Generation Api V2 User Files  Image Id  Trigger Variants Post

post.description:
Upload multiple files in a single request with server-managed concurrency.

    **Limits:**
    - Maximum files per request: Based on subscription tier (10-50)
    - Maximum file size: 100MB per file
    - Files processed with server-managed concurrency

    **Features:**
    - Returns session_id for progress tracking
    - Real-time updates via WebSocket
    - Automatic duplicate detection (optional)

    **Response:**
    - For small batches (<=5 files): Immediate results included
    - For larger batches: Poll status_url for progress

413.description: Too many files or total size exceeds limit

get.description:
Get current status of an upload session.

    Returns:
    - Overall status (pending, processing, completed, etc.)
    - Progress counts (completed, failed, pending)
    - Progress percentage
    - List of recent completions and errors
    - Estimated time remaining (if processing)

Get paginated results from an upload session.

    Returns:
    - List of file results with image_id, status, description, etc.
    - Pagination info
    - Summary statistics

post.description:
Cancel a pending or processing upload session.

    - Stops processing of remaining files
    - Already-processed files keep their results
    - Credits rolled back for unprocessed files

get.description:
List upload sessions for the authenticated tenant.

    - Filter by status
    - Paginated
    - Ordered by created_at desc

parameters.name: upload_method

schema.description: Filter by upload_method: presigned or streaming
schema.title: Upload Method

parameters.description: Filter by upload_method: presigned or streaming

post.description:
Trigger color extraction for an image.

Extracts dominant colors using K-means clustering and stores
results in RGB, HSL, and LAB color spaces.

parameters.name: n_colors

schema.description: Maximum colors to return (up to 16). Actual count is adaptive based on image diversity.
schema.title: N Colors

parameters.description: Maximum colors to return (up to 16). Actual count is adaptive based on image diversity.

get.description:
Get extracted colors for an image.

Returns the color analysis if available, or status if pending/processing.

post.description:
Search images by color.

Supports:
- Hex code with Delta-E tolerance
- Color name matching
- Color family search

get.description:
List available color families with descriptions and examples.

Color families are semantic groupings useful for color-based searches.

schema.title: Response List Color Families Api V2 Colors Families Get

post.description: Queue batch color extraction for multiple images.

Trigger material classification for an image.

Uses VLM providers to detect material types, surface finishes,
textures, and patterns.

get.description:
Get material classification for an image.

Returns the classification if available, or status if pending/processing.

post.description:
Search images by material classification.

Supports filtering by:
- Material type (wood, stone, metal, etc.)
- Surface finish (matte, glossy, brushed, etc.)
- Texture (smooth, rough, grained, etc.)
- Pattern (solid, striped, geometric, etc.)

get.description:
List available material types with descriptions.

These are the material categories detected by the classification system.

schema.title: Response List Material Types Api V2 Materials Types Get

post.description: Queue batch material classification for multiple images.

Initiate OAuth flow for cloud storage connection.

Returns authorization URL to redirect user to.

get.description:
Handle OAuth callback redirect from Google.

Google redirects here with GET after user authorizes.
Returns HTML that posts a message to the popup opener.

post.description:
Complete OAuth flow and create connection.

Called after user authorizes the application.

get.description: List user's cloud storage connections.

Get picker session for Google Picker API.

Returns a session ID that can be used with the picker proxy endpoint.
This keeps access tokens server-side for security (prevents XSS exposure).

post.description:
Proxy API calls for the Google Picker.

Receives a session ID and makes the actual API call server-side,
keeping access tokens secure and not exposed to the frontend.

Import files from cloud storage.

All imports are processed asynchronously via the job queue.

Export files to cloud storage.

Uploads images to the user's connected cloud storage account.

get.description:
Get status of a cloud storage job.

Use this endpoint to track progress of async import/export operations.
Get all folders for the current user as a flat list. Client builds the tree using parent_id relationships.

post.description: Create a new folder at the specified location.

get.description: Get the contents of a folder including subfolders and file count.

delete.description: Delete a folder. Mode 'move_to_parent' moves contents up, 'delete_all' removes everything.

get.description: Get the ancestor chain for a folder for breadcrumb navigation.

post.description: Move a folder to a new parent location.
Move one or more files to a target folder (or root if folder_id is null).

Execute AI-powered image search using the ImageSearchAgent.

This endpoint provides direct access to the search agent without requiring
a chat session. The agent uses ReAct reasoning to interpret your query
and find relevant images.

Returns self-contained results with:
- `results`: Full image objects with metadata
- `summary`: Human-readable summary (refs resolved)
- `summary_raw`: Original summary with [[ref:X]] patterns
- `result_refs`: For building interactive UIs

Execute AI-powered document search using the DocumentSearchAgent.

This endpoint provides direct access to the document search agent without
requiring a chat session. The agent uses ReAct reasoning to interpret your
query and find relevant document sections.

Returns self-contained results with:
- `results`: Full chunk objects with text and metadata
- `document_ids`: Unique documents found
- `summary`: Human-readable summary (refs resolved)
- `result_refs`: For building interactive UIs

Execute AI-powered video search using the VideoSearchAgent.

This endpoint searches across videos and their individual scenes.
Supports browse mode (list videos) and content search (find scenes
matching a query with timestamps).

Returns self-contained results with:
- `results`: Videos with matched scenes and timestamps
- `video_ids`: All matched video IDs
- `summary`: Human-readable summary (refs resolved)
- `result_refs`: For building interactive UIs

Generate a report or summary from given files using the AssistantAgent.

This endpoint provides direct access to the synthesis agent without
requiring a chat session. The agent analyzes provided images and/or
documents to produce a structured markdown report.

Returns:
- `report`: Full markdown report content
- `summary`: Brief summary
- `saved_document_id`: If auto_save was enabled

Perform deep analysis on documents using the DocumentAnalysisAgent.

This endpoint provides direct access to the document analysis agent without
requiring a chat session. The agent can summarize, compare, and categorize
documents.

Returns:
- `analysis`: Full markdown analysis content
- `summary`: Brief summary
- `categorization`: Document categorization if produced
- `chunk_references`: Document chunks cited in the analysis

Organize files into folders using the FolderAgent.

This endpoint provides direct access to the folder organization agent
without requiring a chat session. The agent creates folders and moves
files based on AI-driven categorization.

Returns:
- `summary`: Human-readable summary of actions taken
- `actions`: Detailed list of folder operations performed
- `folders_created`: Count of new folders
- `files_moved`: Count of files moved

Execute a multi-step agent pipeline.

Accepts a list of agent steps with optional explicit dependencies.
Steps are auto-wired by data type when dependencies are not specified.
Execution is parallelized within waves where possible.
Send a support contact message. Public endpoint, no auth required.

get.description: PEP 503 root: list available packages.
PEP 503 package page: list available wheel files with hashes.
Serve a wheel file for pip download.

post.description: Start a multipart video upload and get presigned URLs for chunks
Start multipart uploads for multiple videos in a single request
Confirm successful upload of a video chunk
Finalize multipart upload and trigger processing
Cancel upload and cleanup resources

get.description: Get real-time upload progress

post.description: Get new presigned URL for retrying a failed chunk

get.description:
Serve a scene thumbnail JPEG from S3.

Returns the thumbnail image for a specific video scene,
generated at the scene's midpoint during analysis.

List all scenes for a video with metadata.

Returns scenes ordered by scene_index with timestamps,
descriptions, tags, and thumbnail URLs.
Get the status and progress of a video analysis job
Get the latest analysis job status for a video by its media ID

post.description: Retry a failed or cancelled video analysis job
Manually queue a video for analysis

CreateAPIKeyRequest.title: CreateAPIKeyRequest
CreateAPIKeyRequest.description:
Request model for creating a new API key.

Scopes define the permissions for the API key:
- Role-based scopes: 'read', 'write', 'admin'
- Resource-specific scopes: 'resource:permission' format
  Examples: 'api_keys:read', 'usage:read'

If no scopes are specified, the API key inherits the creator's permissions (capped at admin).
